key_insight: The synthesis of layered ternary iron nitrides FeMN2 (M = W, Mo) reveals distinct structural and electronic properties influenced by composition.
engineering_relevance: Understanding the composition-dependent behavior of these materials can lead to advancements in electronic and magnetic applications.
method: Reactive sputtering of FeMN2 thin films.
Characterization using synchrotron GIWAXS and XAS.
evidence_signal: Both FeWN2 and FeMoN2 exhibit distinct phase purity and microstructure behaviors across various compositions.
watch_item: Phase purity and microstructure behavior in FeMoN2 is composition-sensitive, which may affect its practical applications.
paper_title: Composition-dependent Thin-film Synthesis of Layered Ternary Iron Nitrides FeMN2 (M = W, Mo)
